message.content:
Create lenses/RBRV-RegimeVessel.adoc specification document for vessel regime configuration.

## Context
You're creating a new AsciiDoc spec file for vessel configuration variables (RBRV regime). Follow the pattern established in lenses/RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc.

## Required Work

1. **Read pattern file**: lenses/RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc
   - Note the structure: mapping section â†’ feature groups (tables) â†’ term definitions
   - Note the modern MCM anchor pattern (no `term_` prefix in attributes)

2. **Create lenses/RBRV-RegimeVessel.adoc** with these sections:
   - Document title: "Recipe Bottle Vessel Configuration Regime Specification"
   - Mapping section following RBRN style
   - Feature groups with variable documentation
   - Term definitions section

3. **Document these 6 RBRV variables** (in this order):
   - RBRV_SIGIL â€” vessel identifier (anchors ark identity)
   - RBRV_DESCRIPTION â€” human-readable description
   - RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE â€” path to Dockerfile
   - RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT â€” build context directory
   - RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS â€” target platforms (space-separated)
   - RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY â€” binfmt policy for cross-platform builds

4. **Mapping section guidance**:
   - Use the anchors from above (rbrv_sigil, rbrv_description, rbrv_conjure_dockerfile, etc.)
   - Use modern MCM patterns: `:rbrv_sigil:    <<rbrv_sigil,RBRV_SIGIL>>`
   - Do NOT use term_ prefix in anchors
   - Include relevant category prefix declarations (axrg_ for regime, axf_bash for format)
   - Cross-reference AXLA motifs where appropriate

5. **Variable documentation** (follow RBRN table format):
   - Each variable gets a table row with Purpose, Type, Required, and Constraints
   - Include appropriate type references
   - Keep format/constraints aligned with vessel build concepts

6. **Term definitions section**:
   - Define each of the 6 variables
   - Use the anchors to link from table
   - Make definitions precise and implementation-aware

## Verification Checklist
- [ ] File created at lenses/RBRV-RegimeVessel.adoc
- [ ] Mapping section present with proper syntax
- [ ] All 6 variables documented in feature groups
- [ ] Anchors use modern pattern (no term_ prefix)
- [ ] Term definitions section complete
- [ ] No AsciiDoc syntax errors
- [ ] Attribute reference syntax is correct throughout

Report when complete with verification of all checklist items.

content.text: I'll help you create the RBRV-RegimeVessel.adoc specification document. Let me start by examining the pattern file to understand the structure.

     2â†’:toc:
     3â†’:toc-title: Table of Contents

     5â†’// Begin Mapping Section

     9â†’// at_ prefix: Architectural Term (core system components)

    24â†’:at_sentry_container:        <<term_sentry_container,Sentry Container>>
    25â†’:at_sentry_image:            <<term_sentry_image,Sentry Image>>
    26â†’:at_nameplate:               <<term_nameplate,Nameplate>>
    27â†’:at_nameplate_s:             <<term_nameplate,Nameplates>>

    29â†’:at_workstation:             <<term_workstation,Workstation>>
    30â†’:at_workstation_p:           <<term_workstation,Workstation's>>
    31â†’:at_user:                    <<term_user,User>>

    41â†’:crg_atom_xname:             <<crg_atom_xname,XName>>
    42â†’:crg_atom_fqin:              <<crg_atom_fqin,FQIN>>

    46â†’:crg_atom_cidr:              <<crg_atom_cidr,CIDR>>
    47â†’:crg_atom_domain:            <<crg_atom_domain,Domain>>
    48â†’:crg_atom_port:              <<crg_atom_port,Port>>
    49â†’:crg_list_cidr:              <<crg_list_cidr,CIDRList>>
    50â†’:crg_list_domain:            <<crg_list_domain,DomainList>>
    51â†’
    52â†’// end::mapping-section[]
    53â†’
    54â†’== Overview
    55â†’
    56â†’This Configuration Regime defines requirements for
    57â†’{at_bottle_service}
    58â†’deployment, specifying how
    59â†’{at_nameplate_s}
    60â†’configure container deployment and network security settings.
    61â†’
    62â†’== Feature Groups
    63â†’
    64â†’=== Core Service Identity
    65â†’
    66â†’[cols="1,1,3"]
    67â†’|===
    68â†’|Assignment Variable |Attribute |Details
    69â†’
    70â†’.4+|**`RBRN_MONIKER`**
    71â†’|Purpose|
    72â†’Unique moniker for
    73â†’{at_bottle_service}
    74â†’instance
    75â†’|Type|
    76â†’{crg_atom_xname}:
    77â†’2-12 characters
    78â†’|Required|
    79â†’Yes
    80â†’|Constraints|
    81â†’.4+|**`RBRN_DESCRIPTION`**
    82â†’|Purpose|
    83â†’Human-readable description of service purpose
    84â†’|Type|
    85â†’{crg_atom_string}:
    86â†’0-120 characters
    87â†’|Required|
    88â†’No
    89â†’|Constraints|
    90â†’
    91â†’.4+|**`RBRN_RUNTIME`**
    92â†’|Purpose|
    93â†’Container runtime to use for service deployment
    94â†’|Type|
    95â†’{crg_atom_string}:
    96â†’"docker" or "podman"
    97â†’|Required|
    98â†’Yes
    99â†’|Constraints|
   100â†’Must be either "docker" or "podman"
   101â†’|===
   102â†’
   103â†’=== Container Image Configuration
   104â†’
   105â†’[cols="1,1,3"]
   106â†’|===
   107â†’|Assignment Variable |Attribute |Details
   108â†’
   109â†’.4+|**`RBRN_SENTRY_MONIKER`**
   110â†’|Purpose|
   111â†’Image moniker for
   112â†’{at_sentry_image}
   113â†’|Type|
   114â†’{crg_atom_fqin}:
   115â†’1-128 characters
   116â†’|Required|
   117â†’Yes
   118â†’|Constraints|
   119â†’
   120â†’.4+|**`RBRN_BOTTLE_MONIKER`**
   121â†’|Purpose|
   122â†’Image moniker for
   123â†’{at_bottle_image}
   124â†’|Type|
   125â†’{crg_atom_fqin}:
   126â†’1-128 characters
   127â†’|Required|
   128â†’Yes
   129â†’|Constraints|
   130â†’
   131â†’.4+|**`RBRN_SENTRY_IMAGE_TAG`**
   132â†’|Purpose|
   133â†’Version tag for
   134â†’{at_sentry_image}
   135â†’|Type|
   136â†’{crg_atom_fqin}:
   137â†’1-128 characters
   138â†’|Required|
   139â†’Yes
   140â†’|Constraints|
   141â†’
   142â†’.4+|**`RBRN_BOTTLE_IMAGE_TAG`**
   143â†’|Purpose|
   144â†’Version tag for
   145â†’{at_bottle_image}
   146â†’|Type|
   147â†’{crg_atom_fqin}:
   148â†’1-128 characters
   149â†’|Required|
   150â†’Yes
   151â†’|Constraints|
   152â†’|===
   153â†’
   154â†’=== Entry Service Configuration
   155â†’
   156â†’[cols="1,1,3"]
   157â†’|===
   158â†’|Assignment Variable |Attribute |Details
   159â†’
   160â†’.4+|**`RBRN_ENTRY_ENABLED`**
   161â†’|Purpose|
   162â†’Enable flag for user-initiated entry functionality
   163â†’|Type|
   164â†’{crg_atom_bool}
   165â†’|Required|
   166â†’Yes
   167â†’|Constraints|
   168â†’
   169â†’.4+|**`RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION`**
   170â†’|Purpose|
   171â†’External port exposed on
   172â†’{at_transit_network}
   173â†’for user entry
   174â†’|Type|
   175â†’{crg_atom_port}
   176â†’|Required|
   177â†’When ENTRY_ENABLED=1
   178â†’|Constraints|
   179â†’Must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
   180â†’
   181â†’.4+|**`RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE`**
   182â†’|Purpose|
   183â†’Port used between
   184â†’{at_sentry_container}
   185â†’and
   186â†’{at_bottle_container}
   187â†’for entry traffic
   188â†’|Type|
   189â†’{crg_atom_port}
   190â†’|Required|
   191â†’When ENTRY_ENABLED=1
   192â†’|Constraints|
   193â†’Must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
   194â†’|===
   195â†’
   196â†’=== {at_enclave_network} Configuration 
   197â†’
   198â†’[cols="1,1,3"]
   199â†’|===
   200â†’|Assignment Variable |Attribute |Details
   201â†’
   202â†’.4+|**`RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP`**
   203â†’|Purpose|
   204â†’Base IPv4 address that defines the enclave network range
   205â†’|Type|
   206â†’{crg_atom_ipv4}
   207â†’|Required|
   208â†’Yes
   209â†’|Constraints|
   210â†’
   211â†’.4+|**`RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK`**
   212â†’|Purpose|
   213â†’Network mask width for the enclave network addressing
   214â†’|Type|
   215â†’{crg_atom_decimal}:
   216â†’8-30
   217â†’|Required|
   218â†’Yes
   219â†’|Constraints|
   220â†’
   221â†’.4+|**`RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP`**
   222â†’|Purpose|
   223â†’IP address assigned to the
   224â†’{at_sentry_container}
   225â†’for traffic mediation
   226â†’|Type|
   227â†’{crg_atom_ipv4}
   228â†’|Required|
   229â†’Yes
   230â†’|Constraints|
   231â†’
   232â†’.4+|**`RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP`**
   233â†’|Purpose|
   234â†’IP address assigned to the
   235â†’{at_bottle_container}
   236â†’|Type|
   237â†’{crg_atom_ipv4}
   238â†’|Required|
   239â†’Yes
   240â†’|Constraints|
   241â†’|===
   242â†’
   243â†’=== Uplink Configuration
   244â†’
   245â†’[cols="1,1,3"]
   246â†’|===
   247â†’|Assignment Variable |Attribute |Details
   248â†’
   249â†’.4+|**`RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN`**
   250â†’|Purpose|
   251â†’Minimum port number for bottle-initiated outbound connections
   252â†’|Type|
   253â†’{crg_atom_port}
   254â†’|Required|
   255â†’Yes
   256â†’|Constraints|
   257â†’Must be greater than any Entry port
   258â†’
   259â†’.4+|**`RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_ENABLED`**
   260â†’|Purpose|
   261â†’Enable protected DNS resolution for bottle-initiated requests
   262â†’|Type|
   263â†’{crg_atom_bool}
   264â†’|Required|
   265â†’Yes
   266â†’|Constraints|
   267â†’
   268â†’.4+|**`RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_ENABLED`**
   269â†’|Purpose|
   270â†’Enable protected IP access for bottle-initiated connections
   271â†’|Type|
   272â†’{crg_atom_bool}
   273â†’|Required|
   274â†’Yes
   275â†’|Constraints|
   276â†’
   277â†’.4+|**`RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_GLOBAL`**
   278â†’|Purpose|
   279â†’Enable unrestricted DNS resolution for bottle-initiated requests
   280â†’|Type|
   281â†’{crg_atom_bool}
   282â†’|Required|
   283â†’Yes
   284â†’|Constraints|
   285â†’
   286â†’.4+|**`RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_GLOBAL`**
   287â†’|Purpose|
   288â†’Enable unrestricted IP access for bottle-initiated connections
   289â†’|Type|
   290â†’{crg_atom_bool}
   291â†’|Required|
   292â†’Yes
   293â†’|Constraints|
   294â†’
   295â†’.4+|**`RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS`**
   296â†’|Purpose|
   297â†’CIDR ranges for allowed bottle-initiated outbound traffic
   298â†’|Type|
   299â†’{crg_list_cidr}
   300â†’|Required|
   301â†’When UPLINK_ACCESS_ENABLED=1 and UPLINK_ACCESS_GLOBAL=0
   302â†’|Constraints|
   303â†’
   304â†’.4+|**`RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS`**
   305â†’|Purpose|
   306â†’Domain names allowed for bottle-initiated DNS resolution
   307â†’|Type|
   308â†’{crg_list_domain}
   309â†’|Required|
   310â†’When UPLINK_DNS_ENABLED=1 and UPLINK_DNS_GLOBAL=0
   311â†’|Constraints|
   312â†’|===
   313â†’
   314â†’=== {st_volume_mount} Configuration
   315â†’
   316â†’[cols="1,1,3"]
   317â†’|===
   318â†’|Assignment Variable |Attribute |Details
   319â†’
   320â†’.4+|**`RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS`**
   321â†’|Purpose|
   322â†’Podman volume mount arguments for
   323â†’{at_bottle_container}
   324â†’|Type|
   325â†’{crg_atom_string}:
   326â†’0-240 characters
   327â†’|Required|
   328â†’No
   329â†’|Constraints|
   330â†’|===
   331â†’
   332â†’== Core Term Definitions
   333â†’
   334â†’[[term_bottle_service]]
   335â†’{at_bottle_service}::
   336â†’A complete service instance consisting of one 
   337â†’{at_bottle_container} 
   338â†’and one 
   339â†’{at_sentry_container}, 
   340â†’working together to provide secure functionality.
   341â†’
   342â†’[[term_bottle_container]]
   343â†’{at_bottle_container}::
   344â†’A container that runs the actual service functionality, connected only to the 
   345â†’{at_enclave_network} 
   346â†’and configured with specific 
   347â†’{st_volume_mount_s}.
   348â†’
   349â†’[[term_bottle_image]]
   350â†’{at_bottle_image}::
   351â†’A container image that contains the service functionality intended to run within a 
   352â†’{at_bottle_container}.
   353â†’
   354â†’[[term_sentry_container]]
   355â†’{at_sentry_container}::
   356â†’A privileged container that enforces network security policies for a 
   357â†’{at_bottle_service}, 
   358â†’controlling all network traffic and providing DNS services.
   359â†’
   360â†’[[term_sentry_image]]
   361â†’{at_sentry_image}::
   362â†’A container image that contains the security enforcement functionality for a 
   363â†’{at_sentry_container}.
   364â†’
   365â†’[[term_nameplate]]
   366â†’{at_nameplate}::
   367â†’A configuration file that defines all aspects of a 
   368â†’{at_bottle_service},
   369â†’including its images, network settings, and security policies.
   370â†’
   371â†’[[term_moniker]]
   372â†’{at_moniker}::
   373â†’A unique identifier that distinguishes each 
   374â†’{at_bottle_service} 
   375â†’instance usable on a given
   376â†’{at_workstation}.
   377â†’
   378â†’[[term_workstation]]
   379â†’{at_workstation}::
   380â†’The local computing environment where the 
   381â†’{at_user} 
   382â†’runs containerized services.
   383â†’
   384â†’[[term_transit_network]]
   385â†’{at_transit_network}::
   386â†’The network interface that connects a 
   387â†’{at_sentry_container} 
   388â†’to the
   389â†’{at_workstation_p}
   390â†’network resources, handling both user-initiated entry traffic
   391â†’(in lower port space)
   392â†’and bottle-initiated uplink traffic
   393â†’(in higher port space).
   394â†’
   395â†’[[term_enclave_network]]
   396â†’{at_enclave_network}::
   397â†’An isolated network connecting a 
   398â†’{at_bottle_container} 
   399â†’to its 
   400â†’{at_sentry_container}.
   401â†’
   402â†’[[term_port_map]]
   403â†’{st_port_map}::
   404â†’A specification of how network ports should be exposed and mapped between 
   405â†’{at_sentry_container} 
   406â†’and 
   407â†’{at_bottle_container}.
   408â†’
   409â†’[[term_volume_mount]]
   410â†’{st_volume_mount}::
   411â†’A configuration that allows 
   412â†’{at_bottle_container_s} 
   413â†’to access specified portions of the host filesystem.
   414â†’
   415â†’
   416â†’

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.text: Perfect! Now I understand the pattern. Let me check if RBRV-RegimeVessel.adoc already exists, then create it with the proper structure.

Good, the file doesn't exist yet. Now I'll create the RBRV-RegimeVessel.adoc specification document following the pattern I found in RBRN. I'll include proper mapping section, feature groups, and term definitions for the 6 vessel configuration variables.

input.content:
= Recipe Bottle Vessel Configuration Regime Specification
:toc:
:toc-title: Table of Contents

// Begin Mapping Section
// tag::mapping-section[]

// Category declarations:
// axrg_ prefix: Architecture Regime (configuration regime terms)
// axf_ prefix: Architecture Format (data type and format concepts)
// axm_ prefix: Architecture Mechanism (operational concepts)

// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
//   _ed: past tense (verbs)
//   _ing: progressive form (verbs)

// Domain declaration: <canonical_term>

:axrg_vessel:                <<rbrv_vessel,Vessel>>
:axrg_vessel_s:              <<rbrv_vessel,Vessels>>
:axrg_vessel_identity:       <<rbrv_sigil,Vessel Identity>>
:axrg_conjure_build:         <<rbrv_conjure_build,Vessel Build>>
:axrg_conjure_context:       <<rbrv_conjure_context,Build Context>>
:axrg_platforms:             <<rbrv_platforms,Target Platforms>>
:axrg_binfmt_policy:         <<rbrv_binfmt_policy,Binary Format Policy>>

:axf_bash_string:            <<axf_bash_string,Bash String>>
:axf_bash_path:              <<axf_bash_path,Bash Path>>
:axf_bash_multivalue:        <<axf_bash_multivalue,Space-Separated Values>>
:axf_identifier:             <<axf_identifier,Identifier>>

:crg_atom_string:            <<crg_atom_string,String>>
:crg_atom_xname:             <<crg_atom_xname,XName>>
:crg_atom_fqin:              <<crg_atom_fqin,FQIN>>

// end::mapping-section[]

== Overview

This Configuration Regime defines requirements for
{axrg_vessel_s}
in the Recipe Bottle system, specifying container image build parameters and deployment identifiers for vessel conjuring operations.

== Feature Groups

=== Vessel Identity

[cols="1,1,3"]
|===
|Assignment Variable |Attribute |Details

.4+|**`RBRV_SIGIL`**
|Purpose|
Unique identifier that anchors {axrg_vessel_identity}
for ark registration
|Type|
{crg_atom_xname}:
1-32 characters
|Required|
Yes
|Constraints|
Must be globally unique within the Recipe Bottle registry

.4+|**`RBRV_DESCRIPTION`**
|Purpose|
Human-readable description of vessel purpose and functionality
|Type|
{crg_atom_string}:
0-240 characters
|Required|
No
|Constraints|
May be empty; provides documentation context
|===

=== Vessel Build Configuration

[cols="1,1,3"]
|===
|Assignment Variable |Attribute |Details

.4+|**`RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE`**
|Purpose|
Path to Dockerfile source for {axrg_conjure_build}
|Type|
{axf_bash_path}:
1-256 characters
|Required|
Yes
|Constraints|
Must be valid filesystem path; typically relative to build context root

.4+|**`RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT`**
|Purpose|
Build context directory for container image build operations
|Type|
{axf_bash_path}:
1-256 characters
|Required|
Yes
|Constraints|
Must be accessible directory containing Dockerfile and referenced resources

.4+|**`RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS`**
|Purpose|
Target platforms for {axrg_conjure_build}
|Type|
{axf_bash_multivalue}:
space-separated platform identifiers
|Required|
Yes
|Constraints|
Examples: "linux/amd64", "linux/arm64", "linux/amd64 linux/arm64"

.4+|**`RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY`**
|Purpose|
Binary format policy for cross-platform {axrg_conjure_build} operations
|Type|
{axf_bash_string}:
"native" or "qemu"
|Required|
Yes
|Constraints|
"native" for single-architecture builds; "qemu" for multi-platform emulation
|===

== Core Term Definitions

[[rbrv_vessel]]
{axrg_vessel}::
A configured container image specification within Recipe Bottle, including identity, description, and build parameters for generating deployable artifacts.

[[rbrv_sigil]]
{axrg_vessel_identity}::
The unique identifier ({crg_atom_xname}) that registers a
{axrg_vessel}
in the Recipe Bottle ark, enabling reference by other configuration regimes and deployment orchestration.

[[rbrv_conjure_build]]
{axrg_conjure_build}::
The container image build operation performed by buildx or equivalent multi-platform builder, transforming source code and Dockerfile into deployable
{axrg_vessel}
artifacts.

[[rbrv_conjure_context]]
{axrg_conjure_context}::
The filesystem directory containing the Dockerfile and all resources required for the
{axrg_conjure_build}
operation, passed as the build context to the container image builder.

[[rbrv_platforms]]
{axrg_platforms}::
The enumeration of CPU architectures and operating systems for which the
{axrg_vessel}
shall be built, specified as space-separated platform identifiers (e.g., "linux/amd64 linux/arm64").

[[rbrv_binfmt_policy]]
{axrg_binfmt_policy}::
The execution strategy for cross-platform
{axrg_conjure_build}
operations: "native" for single-platform optimization or "qemu" for emulated multi-platform support.

[[axf_bash_string]]
{axf_bash_string}::
A Bash-compatible string value without embedded whitespace or special characters requiring escaping.

[[axf_bash_path]]
{axf_bash_path}::
A filesystem path suitable for use in Bash scripts, absolute or relative to a known context directory.

[[axf_bash_multivalue]]
{axf_bash_multivalue}::
Multiple values separated by spaces, suitable for iteration in Bash for loops or parameter expansion.

[[axf_identifier]]
{axf_identifier}::
A unique name or code that distinguishes an entity, following naming conventions appropriate to its namespace.

[[crg_atom_string]]
{crg_atom_string}::
An atomic configuration value representing a text string with specified length constraints.

[[crg_atom_xname]]
{crg_atom_xname}::
An atomic configuration value representing a unique identifier conforming to cross-system naming conventions (alphanumeric, underscores, hyphens).

[[crg_atom_fqin]]
{crg_atom_fqin}::
An atomic configuration value representing a fully-qualified image name for container registries.
